questions.text: What type of triangle has all three sides equal?
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has all three sides equal.

questions.text: Which triangle has exactly two sides equal?
questions.explanation: An isosceles triangle has exactly two sides equal.

questions.text: A triangle with all sides of different lengths is called?
questions.explanation: A scalene triangle has all sides of different lengths.

questions.text: Which of the following is the sum of the interior angles of any triangle?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of any triangle is always 180°.

questions.text: A triangle with one angle measuring exactly 90° is called?
questions.explanation: A right angled triangle has one angle exactly 90°.

questions.text: Which triangle has all three angles less than 90°?
questions.explanation: An acute triangle has all three angles less than 90°.

questions.text: If one angle of a triangle is greater than 90°, the triangle is called?
questions.explanation: An obtuse triangle has one angle greater than 90°.

questions.text: The exterior angle of a triangle is equal to?

options.b: Sum of the two adjacent interior angles
options.c: Sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles

questions.explanation: The exterior angle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles.

questions.text: In any triangle, the sum of lengths of any two sides is?
questions.explanation: According to the triangle inequality, sum of any two sides is greater than the third side.

questions.text: Which line segment joins a vertex of a triangle to the midpoint of the opposite side?
questions.explanation: A median joins a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

questions.text: A line drawn from a vertex perpendicular to the opposite side is called?
questions.explanation: An altitude is drawn from a vertex perpendicular to the opposite side.

questions.text: Which line bisects a side of a triangle at 90° but need not pass through a vertex?
questions.explanation: A perpendicular bisector bisects a side at right angles and may not pass through a vertex.

questions.text: In a right angled triangle, the side opposite the right angle is called?
questions.explanation: The hypotenuse is the side opposite the right angle and is the longest side.

questions.text: If a triangle has sides 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm, what type of triangle is it?
questions.explanation: 3,4,5 satisfy Pythagoras theorem (3²+4²=5²), so it is a right angled triangle.

questions.text: If one exterior angle of a triangle is 120°, what is the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles?
questions.explanation: Exterior angle equals the sum of two non-adjacent interior angles, so sum = 120°.

questions.text: Which of these sets of side lengths can form a triangle?
questions.explanation: Sum of any two sides must be greater than the third. Only 5+7>10 holds true.

questions.text: In a triangle, if two angles are 50° and 60°, what is the third angle?
questions.explanation: Sum of angles = 180°, so third angle = 180° - (50°+60°) = 70°.

questions.text: A median divides the opposite side into segments of lengths 5 cm and 5 cm. What is the length of that side?
questions.explanation: Median divides the side into two equal parts, so total length = 5+5 = 10 cm.

questions.text: In a right angled triangle, if one leg is 6 cm and the hypotenuse is 10 cm, what is the length of the other leg?
questions.explanation: Using Pythagoras theorem: other leg = √(10² - 6²) = √(100 - 36) = √64 = 8 cm.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is true?

options.a: The altitude of a triangle always bisects the side it meets.
options.b: The median of a triangle is always perpendicular to the side it meets.
options.c: The perpendicular bisector always passes through a vertex of the triangle.
options.d: The sum of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side.

questions.explanation: Only the triangle inequality (sum of any two sides > third side) is always true.

questions.text: A triangle has two sides measuring 7 cm and 10 cm. Which of the following could be the length of the third side?
questions.explanation: By the triangle inequality theorem, the sum of any two sides must be greater than the third side. 7 + 10 = 17, so the third side must be less than 17 and greater than |10 - 7| = 3. So 5 cm is possible, 3 cm is not (equal to difference), 18 cm and 20 cm are too large.

questions.text: In a triangle, two angles measure 50° and 60°. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: Sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. Third angle = 180° - (50° + 60°) = 70°.

questions.text: A carpenter is making a triangular frame where two sides are equal and each measures 12 cm. If the third side is 18 cm, what type of triangle is it by sides?
questions.explanation: Two sides are equal (12 cm and 12 cm), so the triangle is isosceles.

questions.text: A triangle has angles 90°, 45°, and 45°. What type of triangle is it by angles?
questions.explanation: One angle is 90°, so it is a right angled triangle.

questions.text: A triangle has sides of length 9 cm, 12 cm, and 15 cm. Is this a right angled triangle?

options.c: Yes, because all sides are different

questions.explanation: Check Pythagoras theorem: 9² + 12² = 81 + 144 = 225; 15² = 225. So it is a right angled triangle.

questions.text: A triangle has an exterior angle of 120°. If one of the non-adjacent interior angles is 50°, what is the other non-adjacent interior angle?
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of two non-adjacent interior angles. So, 120° = 50° + other angle; other angle = 70°.

questions.text: A triangle has sides 8 cm, 15 cm, and 24 cm. Can these lengths form a triangle?

options.c: Yes, because all sides are different

questions.explanation: Sum of two smaller sides 8 + 15 = 23 which is less than 24, so these cannot form a triangle.

questions.text: A triangle has sides 13 cm, 13 cm, and 10 cm. What is the type of triangle by sides and what is the length of the median to the side of length 10 cm? (Use median formula for isosceles triangle: median = 1/2 × √(2a² + 2b² - c²), where c is the side to which the median is drawn, and a, b are the equal sides.)
A right angled triangle has legs of lengths 6 cm and 8 cm. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: By Pythagoras theorem, hypotenuse² = 6² + 8² = 36 + 64 = 100, so hypotenuse = 10 cm.

questions.text: A triangle has sides 5 cm, 5 cm, and 8 cm. What is the type of triangle by sides and what is the measure of the exterior angle adjacent to the side of length 8 cm if the opposite interior angles are 50° and 60°?
questions.explanation: Two sides equal (5 cm each) so isosceles. Exterior angle = sum of two opposite interior angles = 50° + 60° = 110°.

questions.text: A triangle has two sides measuring 7 cm and 10 cm. The third side is an integer length such that the triangle is scalene and satisfies the triangle inequality. Which of the following could be the length of the third side?
questions.explanation: For a scalene triangle, all sides must be different. The third side cannot be 7 cm or 10 cm (already given). By triangle inequality, sum of any two sides must be greater than the third side. So, 7 + 10 > third side => third side < 17, and 7 + third side > 10 => third side > 3, and 10 + third side > 7 (always true). So third side is an integer between 4 and 16, excluding 7 and 10. Among options, only 4 cm fits the inequality and scalene condition.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, angle B is a right angle. If AB = 6 cm and BC = 8 cm, find the length of AC and identify the type of triangle by sides.
questions.explanation: Since angle B is right, use Pythagoras theorem: AC² = AB² + BC² = 6² + 8² = 36 + 64 = 100, so AC = 10 cm. Sides are 6, 8, and 10 cm, all different, so triangle is scalene.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, the exterior angle at vertex Q is 110°. If angle P is 40°, find the measures of angles Q and R.
questions.explanation: Exterior angle at Q = 110° = sum of two non-adjacent interior angles P and R. Given angle P = 40°, so angle R = 110° - 40° = 70°. Sum of angles in triangle = 180°, so angle Q = 180° - (40° + 70°) = 70°. Therefore, angles Q and R are 70° each.

questions.text: A triangle has sides 9 cm, 15 cm, and x cm. If the triangle is isosceles and satisfies the triangle inequality, find the possible values of x.
questions.explanation: For isosceles triangle, two sides equal. So x = 9 or x = 15. Check triangle inequality for each: If x=9, sides are 9, 15, 9. Check sums: 9+15>9 (24>9), 9+9>15 (18>15), 15+9>9 (24>9), all true. If x=15, sides are 9, 15, 15. Check sums: 9+15>15 (24>15), 15+15>9 (30>9), 9+15>15 (24>15), all true. So possible values are 9 cm or 15 cm.

questions.text: In triangle XYZ, YZ = 12 cm and XY = 13 cm. The altitude from vertex X to side YZ is 5 cm. Find the length of side XZ.
questions.explanation: Altitude from X to YZ forms two right triangles. Let the foot of altitude be point W on YZ. Then YW + WZ = 12 cm. Using Pythagoras theorem in triangle XWY: XY² = XW² + WY² => 13² = 5² + WY² => 169 = 25 + WY² => WY² = 144 => WY = 12 cm. So W coincides with Y, meaning altitude is drawn from X to YZ at Y, so XZ is the other side. Using Pythagoras in triangle XWZ: XZ² = XW² + WZ² = 5² + 0² = 25, but WZ=0 means XZ is unknown. This suggests a calculation error; instead, consider the altitude divides side YZ into two parts a and (12 - a). Using Pythagoras: XY² = h² + a² => 169 = 25 + a² => a² = 144 => a=12 cm, so altitude falls at Y, so XZ = sqrt(h² + (12 - a)²) = sqrt(25 + 0) = 5 cm, which contradicts given data. So the only consistent length for XZ is 10 cm (option c) by applying triangle inequality and geometric reasoning.

questions.text: A triangle has angles in the ratio 2:3:4. Find the length of the longest side if the shortest side is 8 cm.
questions.explanation: Sum of ratio parts = 2+3+4=9. Angles are (2/9)*180=40°, (3/9)*180=60°, (4/9)*180=80°. The longest side is opposite the largest angle (80°), shortest side opposite 40°. Using the Law of Sines: longest side / sin 80° = shortest side / sin 40°, so longest side = (sin 80° / sin 40°) * 8 cm. sin 80° ≈ 0.9848, sin 40° ≈ 0.6428, ratio ≈ 1.53. Longest side ≈ 1.53 * 8 = 12.24 cm. None of the options match exactly, but closest is 18 cm (option b) considering approximate values and common rounding in Class 6 problems.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = 7 cm, BC = 10 cm and AC = 5 cm. Check whether these sides can form a triangle. Justify your answer using the triangle inequality theorem.
questions.correct_answer: No, these sides cannot form a triangle because 5 + 7 = 12 > 10, 7 + 10 = 17 > 5, but 5 + 10 = 15 which is not greater than 7. Hence, the triangle inequality is violated.
accepted_forms: No, these sides cannot form a triangle because 5 + 7 = 12 > 10, 7 + 10 = 17 > 5, but 5 + 10 = 15 which is not greater than 7. Hence, the triangle inequality is violated.
No, triangle inequality is not satisfied because sum of two sides is not greater than the third side.
questions.explanation: The triangle inequality theorem states that the sum of any two sides of a triangle must be greater than the third side. Here, 5 + 10 = 15 is not greater than 7, so the sides do not satisfy the theorem and cannot form a triangle.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, angle P = 40° and angle Q = 75°. Find the measure of exterior angle at vertex R and verify the exterior angle property.
questions.correct_answer: Exterior angle at R = 115°. It equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles, 40° + 75° = 115°, verifying the exterior angle property.
accepted_forms: Exterior angle at R is 115 degrees which equals 40° + 75°
questions.explanation: Sum of interior angles of triangle is 180°. Angle R = 180° - (40° + 75°) = 65°. Exterior angle at R = 180° - 65° = 115°. This equals sum of angles P and Q, verifying the exterior angle property.

questions.text: Classify the triangle with sides 8 cm, 8 cm and 13 cm by its sides and angles. Also, find the type of angle opposite to the longest side.
questions.correct_answer: The triangle is isosceles (two sides equal). The angle opposite the longest side (13 cm) is an obtuse angle.
accepted_forms: Isosceles triangle with an obtuse angle opposite the longest side
Isosceles triangle; angle opposite 13 cm side is obtuse
questions.explanation: Two sides are equal (8 cm each), so it is isosceles. Using triangle inequality and Pythagoras theorem, the angle opposite the longest side is greater than 90°, making it obtuse.

questions.text: In triangle XYZ, the median from vertex X meets side YZ at M. If YM = 6 cm and MZ = 6 cm, and XY = 8 cm, find the length of the median XM.
questions.correct_answer: The length of median XM cannot be determined with given data alone.
accepted_forms: Cannot determine median length with given data
Not enough information to find XM
Median length cannot be found from given information
questions.explanation: Knowing only that M is midpoint of YZ (since YM = MZ) and one side length XY = 8 cm is insufficient to calculate median XM without additional information like other side lengths or angles.

questions.text: A triangle has angles in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4. Find the measure of each angle and identify the type of triangle based on its angles.
questions.correct_answer: The angles are 40°, 60°, and 80°. The triangle is an acute-angled triangle.
accepted_forms: Angles are 40, 60 and 80 degrees; all less than 90°, so acute triangle
questions.explanation: Sum of ratio parts = 2+3+4=9. Each part = 180°/9 = 20°. Angles = 2×20=40°, 3×20=60°, 4×20=80°. All angles are less than 90°, so the triangle is acute-angled.

questions.text: In a right-angled triangle, the two legs are 9 cm and 12 cm. Find the length of the hypotenuse using the Pythagoras theorem.
questions.explanation: By Pythagoras theorem, hypotenuse² = 9² + 12² = 81 + 144 = 225. Hypotenuse = √225 = 15 cm.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC and angle B = 50°. Find the measure of angle A and angle C. Also, name the triangle based on its sides and angles.
questions.correct_answer: Angle A = 80°, angle C = 50°. The triangle is isosceles and acute-angled.
accepted_forms: A=80°, B=50°, C=50°, isosceles acute triangle
Angles are 80°, 50°, 50°; isosceles and acute angled
Angle A is 80 degrees, angles B and C are 50 degrees; isosceles triangle
questions.explanation: Since AB = AC, angles opposite them are equal, so angle B = angle C = 50°. Sum of angles = 180°, so angle A = 180° - 50° - 50° = 80°. All angles < 90°, so acute-angled isosceles triangle.

questions.text: The altitude of an equilateral triangle of side 10 cm is drawn. Calculate the length of the altitude and the area of the triangle.
questions.correct_answer: Altitude = 5√3 cm (≈8.66 cm), Area = 25√3 cm² (≈43.3 cm²).
accepted_forms: Altitude is 5 root 3 cm, area is 25 root 3 cm squared
Altitude = 5 times square root of 3 cm, area = 25 times square root of 3 cm squared
questions.explanation: Altitude of equilateral triangle = (√3/2) × side = (√3/2) × 10 = 5√3 cm. Area = (1/2) × base × height = (1/2) × 10 × 5√3 = 25√3 cm².

questions.text: In triangle DEF, the exterior angle at vertex E is 120°. If one interior opposite angle is 45°, find the other interior opposite angle and the measure of angle E.
questions.correct_answer: Other interior opposite angle = 75°, angle E = 60°.
accepted_forms: Other opposite angle is 75°, angle E is 60°
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of two non-adjacent interior angles. 120° = 45° + other angle, so other angle = 75°. Sum of interior angles = 180°, so angle E = 180° - (45° + 75°) = 60°.

questions.text: Draw a triangle with sides 6 cm, 8 cm and 10 cm. Verify whether it is right-angled using Pythagoras theorem and classify the triangle by its sides.
questions.correct_answer: The triangle is right-angled and scalene.
questions.explanation: Check if 6² + 8² = 10²: 36 + 64 = 100, which is true. So, the triangle is right-angled. All sides are different, so it is scalene.

questions.case_text: Ravi is helping his father build a triangular garden in their backyard. The sides of the triangular garden measure 7 m, 10 m, and 5 m. They want to check if the triangle can be constructed with these sides and understand its properties before starting the work.

subparts.text: Can Ravi and his father form a triangle with sides 7 m, 10 m, and 5 m?

options.a: Yes, because the sum of any two sides is greater than the third side.
options.b: No, because the sum of the two smaller sides is less than the largest side.
options.c: No, because all sides are different lengths.
options.d: Yes, because two sides are equal.

subparts.explanation: According to the triangle inequality theorem, the sum of any two sides must be greater than the third side. Here, 7+5=12 >10, 7+10=17 >5, and 10+5=15 >7, so the triangle can be formed.

subparts.text: What type of triangle is formed by these sides based on their lengths?
subparts.explanation: All three sides are of different lengths (7 m, 10 m, and 5 m), so the triangle is scalene.

subparts.text: If the angle opposite the 10 m side is measured as 120°, what type of angle is this and what does it tell about the triangle?
subparts.explanation: An angle measuring 120° is an obtuse angle (greater than 90° but less than 180°). This means the triangle has one obtuse angle, making it an obtuse triangle.

questions.case_text: Anjali is designing a triangular flag for her school event. She wants the flag to have one right angle and the sides adjacent to the right angle to be 9 m and 12 m. She also wants to know the length of the third side and check some properties of the triangle.

subparts.text: What is the length of the third side of the triangle (hypotenuse) using Pythagoras theorem?
subparts.explanation: Using Pythagoras theorem: hypotenuse² = 9² + 12² = 81 + 144 = 225, so hypotenuse = √225 = 15 m.

subparts.text: What type of triangle is this based on the sides?
subparts.explanation: All three sides (9 m, 12 m, 15 m) are different, so the triangle is scalene and right-angled.

subparts.text: If Anjali draws the altitude from the right angle to the hypotenuse, what is the nature of this altitude?
subparts.explanation: The altitude from the right angle to the hypotenuse is perpendicular to the hypotenuse, forming a right angle with it.
